title: ENDF/B-VIII.0: The 8th Major Release of the Nuclear Reaction Data Library with CIELO-project Cross Sections, New Standards and Thermal Scattering Data
notes: Primary source for all bundled cross-section and decay data (σ_th, σ_14MeV, t½, gamma lines). Accessed via NNDC Sigma web interface.

title: Conversion Coefficients for use in Radiological Protection against External Radiation
notes: Source for H*(10) ambient dose equivalent conversion coefficients h_K (Sv per Bq·s·m⁻²) used in dose rate calculation in physics.py.

title: Validation of JENDL-4.0 Library for Fusion Neutronics Using FNS Benchmark Experiments
journal: Journal of Nuclear Science and Technology
notes: JAEA Fusion Neutronics Source (FNS) benchmark facility. Premier experimental dataset for 14.1 MeV activation of concrete and steel. Used to validate activation calculations in the literature review.

title: Development of Low-Activation Concrete for Fusion Reactor
authors: M. Kinno, T. Kusunoki, K. Kawasaki, et al.
notes: Describes the JAEA/Fujita low-activation concrete (LAC) program. Fused alumina aggregate + high-alumina cement (HAC). ΣDi/Ci reduction ratios cited: ~1/300–1/400 vs. andesite baseline for 14 MeV flux.

title: Best Practices for Characterization of Activated Metals at DOE Nuclear Facilities
notes: Activated metals characterization best practices. Provides dose driver ranking for SS304 (Co-60, Mn-54, Fe-55, Ni-63, Eu-152) and Cu (Co-60, Zn-65, Ni-63). Co-60 from ~500–2000 ppm Co impurity identified as dominant driver at 2–5 y post-irradiation (60–95% of external dose).

title: Activation and Dose Rate Calculations for the ITER Bioshield
notes: ITER bioshield study. Eu-152 from Eu traces in OPC concrete identified as dominant long-term dose driver (Eu-151 σ_th ~9200 b Maxwellian average vs 5900 b 2200 m/s). Co-60 and Cs-134 also significant.

title: Decommissioning and Radioactive Waste Management for Fusion Facilities
notes: IAEA guidance on fusion facility decommissioning and waste management. NRC Class A/B/C limits and IAEA RS-G-1.7 clearance levels. Referenced for waste classification discussion.

title: 10 CFR Part 61 — Licensing Requirements for Land Disposal of Radioactive Waste
notes: NRC Class A/B/C activity concentration limits. Key values: Ni-63 Class C limit 3.5–35 Ci/m³ (dominant waste driver for Ni-bearing alloys), Co-60 Class C 700 Ci/m³, Nb-94 Class C 0.2 Ci/m³ (most restrictive single limit for activated metals).

title: Application of the Concepts of Exclusion, Exemption and Clearance
notes: IAEA clearance levels. Key values: Co-60 0.1 Bq/g, Eu-152 0.1 Bq/g, Cs-134 0.1 Bq/g, Ni-63 100 Bq/g, Fe-55 1000 Bq/g. Used for future waste classification plot overlays (Phase 4).

title: EUROFER97: A Low Activation Ferritic-Martensitic Steel for Fusion Applications
notes: EUROFER97 composition (9Cr-1W-0.2V-0.1Ta, balance Fe). Eliminates Ni, Mo, Nb, Cu intentionally for low activation. Enables shallow land burial within 50–100 y cooling.

title: NuDat 3 — Nuclear Structure and Decay Data
notes: Online interface for decay data, gamma intensities, half-lives. Cross-checked against ENDF/B-VIII.0 for Co-60, Na-24, Eu-152, Eu-154, Cs-134, Sc-46, Ba-133, Zn-65 gamma lines; also used for Ag-110m, Ag-108m, Ta-182, Nb-94 decay data.

title: Isomeric Cross Section Ratio for the (n,γ) Reaction on ¹⁰⁹Ag at Thermal and 14.1 MeV Neutron Energies
journal: Journal of Nuclear Science and Technology
notes: Measured isomeric partial cross-section for Ag-109(n,γ)→Ag-110m at thermal energies: σ_m = 4.12 ± 0.10 b (total Ag-109 σ_th ≈ 91 b; ~95% branches to short-lived Ag-110g). Primary source for Ag-110m cross-section used in Copper (OFHC) activation.

title: Cobalt Content of Stainless Steels Used in Nuclear Power Plants
authors: VTT Technical Research Centre of Finland
institution: VTT Technical Research Centre of Finland
notes: Measured Co content in commercial SS-304 and SS-316: range 1340–2570 ppm in commercial product, median ~1800 ppm. Nuclear-grade (low-Co SS) specifications: ≤500 ppm (Sandmeyer 304CO), reactor-grade ≤100 ppm. Basis for Co-59 impurity levels in SS-316 commercial vs nuclear-grade variants.

title: ASTM B170 — Standard Specification for Oxygen-Free Electrolytic Copper — Refinery Shapes
notes: Specifies composition limits for C10100 (OFE) copper: Ag ≤25 ppm, Zn ≤1 ppm. Corrects prior assumption of 10–100 ppm Zn in OFHC Cu. At these limits, Ag-110m (from Ag-109(n,γ)) dominates over Zn-65 as medium-term dose driver with ~85× higher σ·f at thermal energies.

title: Tungsten as a Structural Divertor Material
journal: IOP Conference Series: Materials Science and Engineering
notes: ITER-grade tungsten specification: Ta ≤50 ppm, Co ≤5–10 ppm, total metallic impurities ≤100 ppm. Industrial W (lamp/HV grade): Ta up to 500 ppm. Ta-181(n,γ)→Ta-182 (σ_th=20.5 b, t½=114.74 d) is the dominant medium-term activation product in W after the Re/Os chain. Basis for Ta-181 levels in Tungsten (ITER-grade impurities) and Tungsten (industrial impurities) materials.

title: EUROFER97: A Low Activation Ferritic-Martensitic Steel for Fusion Applications
authors: R. Lindau, A. Möslang, M. Rieth et al.
notes: Full characterization of EUROFER97 composition and activation properties. Demonstrates hands-on decommissioning achievable within 50–100 y due to elimination of Ni, Mo, Nb, Cu. Dominant long-term products: Fe-55, Mn-54, Ta-182 from 0.1 wt% Ta alloying element. Basis for EUROFER97 material composition in data.py.

title: Assessment of Titanium Alloys for Fusion Reactor First-Wall and Blanket Applications
notes: Assessment of Ti alloys (including Ti-6Al-4V) for fusion applications. Identifies tramp impurities (Co, Ni, Ag, Nb) as detrimental for radiological properties. Residual activity dominated by Al-26 (t½=720 ky) at very long cooling times from Al-27(n,2n) at 14 MeV. Confirms reduced-activation character for 10–100 y range.

title: Survey of Radiation Effects in Titanium Alloys
notes: Survey of radiation damage and activation data for Ti alloys in fusion/nuclear environments. Covers Ti-6Al-4V, Ti-70A, and others. Confirms void swelling resistance and reduced activation. Na-24 from Al-27(n,α) identified as dominant early shutdown dose driver.

title: Performance characteristics and waste implications of WC materials used as neutron shielding materials in fusion
notes: Dedicated analysis of WC neutron shielding waste implications in fusion context. Co binder (3–6 wt%) identified as dominating activation; Fe-based low-activation binder alternatives evaluated. WC has lowest long-term activity among shielding grades without Co binder. Basis for WC-Co material entry and warning notes.

title: Shielding materials in the compact spherical tokamak
journal: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society A
notes: Systematic evaluation of shielding materials for compact fusion devices (ST design). WC, SS, and heavymet evaluated for combined n+γ attenuation. Ni content in heavymet alloys flagged as primary activation/waste concern for high-fluence applications.

title: Nuclear Analysis and Design of Concrete Radiation Shielding for Nuclear Power Plants
notes: Standard concrete composition for radiation shielding analysis. Provides the canonical OPC concrete elemental weight fractions used in the OPC Concrete material entry: H 1.0%, O 52.91%, Na 1.60%, Mg 0.24%, Al 2.05%, Si 20.45%, K 1.92%, Ca 14.29%, Fe 3.44%, C 0.10%.

title: SWX-277 Kretekast Borated Concrete — Material Specification
notes: Kretekast SWX-277 MCNP material definition (m402). Weight fractions: H 4.9%, O 62.66%, B-10 0.308%, B-11 1.297%, N 0.01%, Na 0.13%, Al 21.45%, S 0.16%, Si 1.38%, Ca 6.56%, C 0.45%, Fe 0.27%. Density 1.68 g/cm³. High-alumina borated concrete. Composition provided directly by user from MCNP deck.

title: Dose rates and specific activities of copper based materials irradiated during the TT plasma operation at JET
notes: JET measured activation of Al-Bronze and CuCrZr under D-T plasma neutrons. Confirms Cu-64 (12.7 h), Zn-65 (244 d) as primary early/medium products. Co-60 from Co impurity identified as dominant >1 y dose contributor. Relevant cross-validation for Cu OFHC activation model.

title: Technology, Safety, and Costs of Decommissioning a Reference PWR Power Station
notes: PWR decommissioning reference. Documents A36 structural steel Co impurity levels (50–5000 ppm scrap-origin) and their impact on activated waste classification. Basis for A36 conservative Co=2000 ppm model. Also documents Cu residuals in scrap steel and their Zn-65 activation products.

title: Radioactive Waste Management Guide for Nuclear Power Plant Decommissioning
notes: Identifies Nb-94 (from Nb-93(n,γ), t½=20,300 y) as most restrictive single isotope under NRC 10 CFR 61 for activated metal waste: Class C limit = 0.2 Ci/m³. Even trace Nb (50 ppm in SS-316) can drive waste classification if fluence is high. Used for Nb-93 impurity modeling in SS-316.

title: 10 CFR Part 61: Licensing Requirements for Land Disposal of Radioactive Waste
notes: Primary US regulatory framework for near-surface LLW disposal. Table 1 sets concentration limits for shorter-lived isotopes (Class A/B/C); Table 2 sets Class C limits for longer-lived isotopes including Nb-94 (0.2 Ci/m³), C-14 (8 Ci/m³), Ni-59 (220 Ci/m³), Ni-63 (700 Ci/m³), Tc-99 (3 Ci/m³). Sum-of-fractions rule applies for mixed-isotope packages.
